entry.title: S/4 HANA Cloud Public Editionのワークプロセスの枯渇が検知できない
entry.content:
お世話になっております。

S/4 PERSON_NAME Cloud Public Editionのシステムにおいて、ワークプロセスの枯渇によりジョブ実行ができない事象が発生しました。
オンプレミスのS/4であれば、Tr-cd:SM51を実行することで各ワークプロセスの情報が参照できましたが、S/4 PERSON_NAME Cloud Public Editionでは参照する方法が見当たりません。
なおCloud ALMへ連携しておりますが、Cloud ALM for OperaionsのHealth Montioringでもワークプロセスの情報が参照できない(メトリクスに項目がない)です。
メトリックにUsed Dialog and Batch Work Processesという項目があり、閾値を設定できるようですが、通常何個ワークプロセスが動いているかを知ることができないため、何を閾値にすれば良いかも不明です。
また、閾値に達した際に何のアクションができるかも不明です。(Eventsの項目にある内容であればメール通知等できますが、項目がありません。)
ワークプロセスの利用状況を監視する手段をご教示ください。

 

また、閾値に達した際に何のアクションができるかも不明です。(Eventsの項目にある内容であればメール通知等できますが、項目がありません。)
ワークプロセスの利用状況を監視する手段をご教示ください。

 

よろしくお願いします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Cloud Public Editionのワークプロセスの枯渇が検知できない
entry.content:
平素よりお世話になっております。

恐れ入りますが、下記のKBをご参照頂き、メトリックにUsed Dialog and Batch Work Processesの閾値をご設定ください。
メトリック「Used Dialog and Batch Work Processes」に対して、SAP標準の閾値の推奨はございません。お客様に自由に設定できます。
下記のKBには、80％ -> Warning,  90% - > Critical が設定されているが、お客様自身の場合により、変更できます。 

0003625860　How to monitor used work processes of  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring


また、イベント「High Work Process Usage」を設定することで、アラートやメール通知が設定できます。

宜しくお願いします。
SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: AI Launchpad, Generic Secret Creation Error, Creation...
entry.content:
Correct value for authentication for AWS in this case is NoAuthentication: 
"authentication": "Tm9BdXRoZW50aWNhdGlvbg=", // Base64 encoded value for NoAuthentication
I found this tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-orchestration-grounding..html step no. 5, but this information was not available earlier.

entry.title: Re: I mistakenly gave view selection only on request for...
entry.content:
Hello @PushpaTharshini 

Go to MM01 or MM02 or MM03. Choose menu option Defaults -> Views... A pop up window will display where you can select default views and uncheck "View selection only on request":

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide
entry.content:
Hi all, 

we are currently activating document grounding in our system. We followed all steps and we were able to create the pipeline via Postman. And in this guideline the activation of the pipeline is the last step but if we want to ask joule about information from our documents we get the response that the information is based on general knowledge. 
Do someone know why joule is not getting access to the documents we storage on the sharepoint site?

FYI: we also got the Error 500 by activation the pipeline - we used for the document grounding a functional user with a passwort. In our company the 2FA is a general setting for all user with a password - to solve the problem we have to deactivate the 2FA for that user and after that we got the correct pipeline id.

Kind Regards

entry.title: Re: PO Goods Receipt Exception Task Automation in SAP ByD...
entry.content:
@itaKraus wrote:

Hi i try to extend an BO and make some pdi developments via sdk app i try to create a .xbo for the trigger of Inbound Logistics - Purchase Orders post good receit on save Find the trigger for receiving a purchase order to the warehouse and creating the INBOUND DELIVERY as a result. i try to extend ConfirmedInboundDelivery but i am not sure if this is the right place : my goal is to create .xbo there check some validations like: 1.1. When a warehouse user posts a Purchase Order Goods Receipt (GR), Gauzy requires follow-up in cases where certain exceptions occur (per line): a. Over-Quantity Receipt – The total received quantity is greater than the ordered quantity on the PO line by more than 5%. b. Early Delivery – The receipt is posted before the delivery date that was defined on the PO line by more than month and year. then we have to check the Target logistic area id and do some validations .... the main request is that you try to answer me what is tje correct .xbo that i need to extend in order to check this trigger process i attached a screen shot of the window that i need to exctend

To check validations during the "post goods receipt on save" process for purchase orders in SAP Business ByDesign, the correct Business Object (BO) to extend is ConfirmedInboundDelivery.xbo. This BO is directly involved when a goods receipt is posted, allowing you to implement logic in its BeforeSave or AfterSave events to validate conditions like over-quantity receipt, early delivery, and target logistic area ID against the corresponding purchase order information.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps/Process Automation: "Workflow Definiti...
entry.content: Hello TiagoRibeiro, I'm having trouble adding a required header to the connection between SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Automation. In Postman the required Header solved the problem, but i'm not able to find the place where the header needs to be placed so that the connection between SAP Build Apps und Process Automation works. I've tried adding it to the destination as described in the documentation (https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/http-destinations?q=apikey#custom-query-parameters-and-headers), but the app still fails to start the process. Unfortunately, using a public environment isn't an option for our project, as the notification artifact cannot be deployed there. Could you please provide a different way to add the required header?

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Events in Warsaw - 2025
entry.content: Thank you!

entry.title: Time Zone Change Impact on Timestamp Storage
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,
We are planning to update all our Linux/Unix application servers by using UTC time zone instead of EDT/EST


  • If data was stored in the database while the servers were set to EDT/EST, how will this information be displayed or interpreted after the servers are switched to UTC?
  • When SAP stores timestamped information in the database, does it use the application server’s local time, UTC, or another standard?
  • Are there any additional considerations we should be aware of?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Time Zone Change Impact on Timestamp Storage
entry.content:
Switching your Linux/Unix application servers from EDT/EST to UTC can have several implications for how timestamped data is stored and interpreted. Here are the answers to your questions:

1. Interpretation of Data Stored in EDT/EST After Switching to UTC

If data was stored in the database while the servers were set to EDT/EST, the timestamps will reflect the local time zone (EDT/EST) at the time of storage. After switching the servers to UTC, the interpretation of these timestamps will depend on how your application handles time zones.

  • Display: If your application is designed to display timestamps in the server's local time zone, the timestamps will now be displayed in UTC. This means that a timestamp stored as "2023-10-01 12:00:00 EDT" will be displayed as "2023-10-01 16:00:00 UTC" after the switch.
  • Conversion: If your application converts timestamps to the user's local time zone, it should continue to do so correctly, provided it has access to the original time zone information (EDT/EST).

2. SAP Timestamp Storage

SAP typically stores timestamped information in UTC in the database. This approach ensures consistency and avoids issues related to time zone changes. When data is retrieved, SAP applications convert the UTC timestamps to the local time zone of the application server or the user.

3. Additional Considerations

Here are some additional considerations to keep in mind when switching your servers to UTC:

  • Database Schema: Ensure that your database schema can handle time zone conversions if necessary. Storing timestamps in UTC is generally recommended for consistency.
  • Application Logic: Review your application logic to ensure it correctly handles time zone conversions. This includes both displaying timestamps and processing time-sensitive data.
  • Scheduled Jobs: Update any scheduled jobs or cron tasks to reflect the change in time zone. Jobs scheduled in EDT/EST will need to be adjusted to run at the correct UTC time.
  • Logs and Monitoring: Update logging and monitoring tools to ensure they correctly interpret and display timestamps in UTC.
  • User Communication: Inform users about the change, especially if they rely on timestamped data. Provide guidance on how to interpret timestamps in the new time zone.
  • Testing: Thoroughly test your applications and systems to ensure they function correctly after the time zone change. Pay special attention to any time-sensitive operations.

By considering these factors, you can ensure a smooth transition to UTC and maintain the integrity and usability of your timestamped data.

entry.title: Re: How to Assign Business Partner to Communication Syste...
entry.content:
Hello @sanjaynaiksap 

There’s no standard API to assign a Business Partner to a Communication System in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition; this can only be done manually via the apps. API_BUSINESS_PARTNER handles BP master data, but not system assignments.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Amount field is negative, while GR posting.

entry.title: Collected information about workload classes in context of SAP HANA (with example)
entry.content:
last updated: 2025-06-30

Introduction
There is huge knowledge available about SAP HANA Workload Classes,  but the knowledge is very fragmented (Help Portal, Blog post, SAP KBA, SAP Note, SAP Learning, other). The aim of this article is simplify and make content consumption easier with a centralized page. There is no reason to duplicate the already well prepared content, so the goal is to collect the available knowledge and put it in a well structured format. The logic of the page follows the earlier published articles in the same series.

🎓 The blog contains SAP Learning references. 📺

What is SAP HANA?

Table of Content (The internal links had been removed cause the new blogging platform only supports basic html, and the use of anchor links not supported.) 

  1. Task 1. - Exercise
    1. Prerequisites
    2. Exercise 1.
    3. Exercise 2.
    4. Conclusion

  2. Task 2. - Documentation Library
    1. Where to find documentations in SAP Help Portal?
    2. Which are the master SAP Knowledge Base Articles?
    3. Where to find learning materials?
    4. Managing Workload Classes
    5. Hierarchies of Workload Classes
    6. Which other documentation is important regarding SAP HANA Workload Management?
    7. Which are the related SQL Statement collection reports?
    8. What blog posts can be helpful?
    9. Other articles in this series
    10. Other articles in connected series
    11. Useful bookmark

Task 1. – Exercise

Prerequisites

  • Hypervisor (VMware Workstation Player / VMware Workstation Pro / or any alternatives)
  • SAP HANA, express edition (3573213 - SAP HANA Express Edition - Central)
  • SAP HANA Studio

Number of CPUs Available: 4.

Exercise 1.

The task is to create a workflow class and test its operation. The task is performed using the SAP HANA Studio tool and the SAP HANA Cockpit interface.

  1. In SAP HANA Studio I executed the commands with 'SYSTEM' user and setup the workload class for 'DB_ADMIN' user to manage the 'HDBStudio' related executions. The goal is to reject execution from this user while high CPU usage of the system.
  2. I checked the workload class with 'SYSTEM' user by using "HANA_Workload_WorkloadClasses_" report.
  3. I generated CPU load on the server (with executing different reports in same time).
  4. I executed SQL statement with 'DB_ADMIN' user. It failed.
  5. I checked the workload class, admission control events with 'SYSTEM' user by using "HANA_Workload_AdmissionControlEvents_" report (EVENT_REASON).

Commands:

CREATE WORKLOAD CLASS "WC_HXE_DBADMIN" SET 'PRIORITY' = '0', 'STATEMENT THREAD LIMIT' = '3', 'STATEMENT MEMORY LIMIT' = '4';
ALTER WORKLOAD CLASS "WC_HXE_DBADMIN" SET 'ADMISSION CONTROL REJECT CPU THRESHOLD' = '90';
CREATE WORKLOAD MAPPING "WM_HXE_DBADMIN" WORKLOAD CLASS "WC_HXE_DBADMIN" SET 'APPLICATION NAME' = 'HDBStudio' , 'USER NAME' = 'DB_ADMIN';

Note: first the 'ADMISSION CONTROL REJECT CPU THRESHOLD' value was set to 50 then 90 for testing purposes.

Reference:

  • 3259346 - How to create, modify and remove workload classes and workload mappings

Workload Class in SAP HANA Studio with "HANA_Workload_WorkloadClasses_" report:

Workload Class in SAP HANA Cockpit interface:

During the system had high CPU usage and I started to do execution in SAP HANA Studio tool with 'DB_ADMIN' user. The execution was rejected. It can be checked with "HANA_Workload_AdmissionControlEvents_" report (e.g. in SAP HANA Studio):

Reference:

  • 3583014 - Rejected by workload class configuration with 'Exceeded cpu threshold' error

Exercise 2.

The task is to modify a workflow class and test its operation. The task is performed using the SAP HANA Studio tool and the SAP HANA Cockpit interface.

  1. Execute memory intensive report "HANA_Configuration_MiniChecks_" with DB_ADMIN user with previous workload class setup (Statement Memory Limit = 4). Execution successful.
  2. Modification on the workload class (Statement Memory Limit = 2).
  3. Execute memory intensive report "HANA_Configuration_MiniChecks_" with DB_ADMIN user with modified workload class setup. Execution failed.

Commands:

ALTER WORKLOAD CLASS "WC_HXE_DBADMIN" SET 'PRIORITY' = '0', 'STATEMENT THREAD LIMIT' = '3', 'STATEMENT MEMORY LIMIT' = '2';

Workload Class in SAP HANA Cockpit interface:

The execution which was successful previously now failed.

Note: after resetting the parameter (to value 4), the execution will be successful again.

Conclusion

You can manage workload in SAP HANA by creating workload classes and workload class mappings. Workload classes can be configured in several ways depending on actual needs.

Task 2. – Documentation Library

IMPORTANT !
=================================================================

Where to find documentations in SAP Help Portal?

  • SAP HANA Administration Guide for SAP HANA Platform - Workload Management
  • SAP HANA Administration Guide for SAP HANA Platform - Managing Workload with Workload Classes

Which are the master SAP Knowledge Base Articles?

  • 2222250 - FAQ: SAP HANA Workload Management

Where to find learning materials?

  • SAP Learning - Setting up SAP HANA Workload Management > SAP HANA Workload Classes

=================================================================

Managing Workload Classes
The management of the workload class objects can be achieved with SQL statements/commands and with the  SAP HANA Cockpit tool.

  • SAP HANA Administration with SAP HANA Cockpit - Managing Workload Classes
  • SAP HANA SQL Reference Guide for SAP HANA Platform - Workload Management Statements

Hierarchies of Workload Classes

  • 3247119 - How to use Workload class Hierarchies
  • 3544218 - Using Hierarchies of Workload Classes and their consequences in SAP HANA
  • SAP HANA Administration Guide for SAP HANA Platform - Hierarchies of Workload Classes

Which other documentation is important regarding SAP HANA Workload Management?

  • 3478705 - Workload class and mapping examples
  • 2331857 - SAP HANA workload class support for SAP client applications
  • 3271136 - How to balance CPU threads usage between applications and HANA system
  • 3259346 - How to create, modify and remove workload classes and workload mappings
  • 3583014 - Rejected by workload class configuration with 'Exceeded cpu threshold' error
  • 3580898 - How to Verify Concurrency Limit, STATEMENT THREAD LIMIT Has Been Properly Applied for Workload Classes in SAP HANA

Which are the related SQL Statement collection reports?
The general way to do the monitoring is to use SQL commands. The relevant information can be reviewed with the following reports.

  • 1969700 - SQL Statement Collection for SAP HANA
  • "HANA_Workload_AdmissionControlEvents_" report
  • "HANA_Workload_WorkloadClasses_" report
  • "HANA_Workload_WorkloadClasses_Statistics_" report

What blog posts can be helpful?

  • SAP Community - HANA Workload Management deep dive part I
  • SAP Community - HANA Workload Management deep dive part II
  • SAP Community - HANA Workload Management deep dive part III (Workload Classes)

Other articles in this series
✍️ Collected information about memory in context of SAP HANA
✍️ Collected information regarding partitioning in SAP HANA (with examples)
✍️ Collected information regarding High Availability and Disaster Recovery in SAP HANA
✍️ Collected information belongs to the topics of query execution and optimization in SAP HANA database  
✍️ Collected information about reclaim / shrink / defragmentation topic in context of SAP HANA persiste...

Other articles in connected series
✍️ Where can I find knowledge and information belongs to SAP HANA?
✍️ Where can I find information about the available tools for SAP HANA (all types of use)?

Useful bookmark
🔖 2872774 – Bookmark of Frequently Asked Questions for SAP HANA
🔖 3311408 – Bookmark of SQL Statement Collection reports for SAP HANA
🔖 2658020 – List of SAP HANA SQL Error Codes

Do you have further questions?
Q&A link for SAP HANA: https://answers.sap.com/tags/73554900100700000996

Contribution
If you find any missing information belongs to the topic, please let me know. I am happy to add the new content. My intention is to maintain the content continuously to keep the info up-to-date.

Release Information

Release DateDescription
2025.06.30First/initial Release of the SAP Blog Post documentation (Technical Article).

entry.title: Re: Difference in VA05 output and BAPI_SALESORDER_GETLIST...
entry.content: Now to build the logic to fetch Pending order value , i have created a new Customer in system and created 4 sales documents - 1st- open order , 2nd- Order with Open Delivery , 3rd- orders fully invoice & 4th- order with partial qty delivered , but when i ran VA05 report to check Open orders , it is also considering full quantities of 4th-sales order with partial deliveries along with 1st -Open Order , i am a bit confused that system should display only those quantities whose are open in sales order not delivered

entry.title: Custom action button in S/4HANA Public Cloud.
entry.content:
Hello Community,
I’m working on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud fiori application and I have a requirement to add a custom action button to a standard Fiori application. As we know, direct modification of standard apps is restricted in the public cloud.
Can anyone guide me on the possible extensibility options to achieve this?
Is there any supported way to add a custom button using Developer Extensibility or any?
If not directly possible, is there any standard app developed using RAP BO (Restful ABAP Programming Model) that supports custom action extensions?
Ideally, I want to add some custom button and logic behind that button . Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Automatic Billing Date in Return Order CBAR
entry.content:
Hello @Muzzamil_SD_Consultant 

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, for CBAR Return Orders, the system automatically uses the return or PGR date as the billing date. This is standard behaviour and can't be changed via configuration.

You can adjust the billing date manually, but automation or logic changes would need to go through an SAP Influence Request.

Check the SAP help Reference documentation below
SAP Help – Returns and CBAR Process

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: sap public cloud release of2508
entry.content:
Hello Experts ,

how do we gather information what are changes happend as per module eg:sourcing and procurement in sap public cloud for updates.

how do we check as per our scopes in sap portal what changes that are occuring meanhwile .

 

Kindly guide through this ..

entry.title: SAP CDC - Dataflow Reader Component: Delta Field for Conditional Updates
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a question regarding SAP CDC and how to handle delta fields in the Dataflow Reader component.

Here's the scenario:
We have a custom field on a user/account record. If this field has a value (i.e., it is filled in), we need to read those records and perform an update. I'm using a dataflow scheduled to run once per day.

I'm trying to use the delta field (e.g., lastUpdatedTimestamp) in the Reader component to filter for recently updated records. However, it seems that when I use lastUpdatedTimestamp, it only considers changes within the last 24 hours. As a result, some records are being missed.

For example, if the custom field was filled in on June 25, and the dataflow runs on July 1, the record is not picked up because it falls outside the typical "delta" window.

I’ve tried expressions like:
lastUpdatedTimestamp <= ${now-3d} or ${lastUpdatedTimestamp -3d}, but they don’t seem to give the desired results.

Question:
What is the correct way to configure the delta field so that it picks up records where the custom field was filled in recently, even if the update happened a few days ago? Is there a better approach for handling this in scheduled dataflows?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Request for guidance on certification, test environment, and API package for our SAP integration
entry.content:
We are a Build SAP Partner and would like to request your assistance regarding the next steps toward the certification of our integration with SAP, and the options available to us.
  • Background and Integration Details
Our product, has been integrated with SAP ERP (on-premise) through a set of custom ODATA services implemented in SAP . These services include:
  • ZMM_EMPACT_UOM – Unit of Measure (GET, POST)
  • ZMM_EMPACT_ADDRESS – Address (GET, POST)
  • ZMM_EMPACT_CC – Company Code (GET, POST)
  • ZMM_EMPACT_PLANTCC – Plant (GET, POST)
  • ZMM_EMPACT_MATERIAL – Material (GET, POST)
Using this integration, we successfully demonstrated a bidirectional inventory-related flow from our application into SAP and back. 
Our application has an inventory capability and manages part sets and part instances, and we have established the following logical mapping between SAP entities and product entities:

Product Entity Name
 
SAP Entity Name
 
Address
Address
Organization
Company Code
Site
Plant
Unit Of Measure
Unit Of Measure
Inventory Component Set and Inventory Component Instance
Material
 
These are then pushed into SAP via the custom ODATA services and also retrieved back into our system. If the user makes a change in SAP on those entities, we would like to have ingested the changes from SAP into our product. Right now, this is done by checking the data from SAP and comparing with the current data from AMMS and if there are changes in SAP, we then bring them in AMMS.
 
  • Certification Goal and Migration Need
As discussed previously with the SAP ICC team, we understand that on-premises SAP integrations are no longer eligible for certification, and we are now looking to:
  1. Adapt/migrate our integration to a cloud-compatible SAP technology
  2. Define the appropriate certification scenario
  3. Obtain access to a sandbox/test environment for integration testing
  4. Achieve certification of our bi-directional inventory integration
     
  • What We Have Explored
Prior to developing the custom ODATA services, we explored the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, where we found the SAP Returnable Packaging Management package with the following ODATA/V4 services:
  • Material General Data
  • Material Plant Data
  • Company Code
  • Address
  • Plant
  • Storage Location
  • Unit of Measure
  • Serial Numbers
These services seemed promising, and we noticed that some were even available with sandbox access using SAP ID-based authentication.
 
Would it be possible, following further discussions, to create a dedicated API package in SAP Business Accelerator Hub for our specific use case and integration flow?
Our desired scenario centers around inventory management capabilities, including material, location, unit of measure, and plant-level data.
Is SAP BTP the most suitable platform for this kind of integration and eventual certification? If not, what alternatives would SAP recommend?
Could we be provided access to a test/sandbox environment to develop and validate our new cloud-based integration before submitting it for certification?
 
We are fully committed to aligning our integration with SAP’s current cloud strategy and technical standards and would highly appreciate your guidance on the fastest and most effective path toward certification.
Looking forward to your recommendations.

entry.title: Re: Understanding ABAP Pipeline Engine used for Data Integration in Datasphere
entry.content:
hello,

from what I know the RMS buffer tables have a fixed size (buffer size parameter in Tcode DHRDBMON).

What happens if the buffer is full? 

does the job /1DH/PUSH_CDS_DELTA stop writing rows into the buffer table?

and when the subscriber gets the data and empty the buffer table, does the job /1DH/PUSH_CDS_DELTA restart writing the rows into the buffer? or do we need to maintain manually and do the "reset write flag" ?

Thanks,

Alberto

entry.title: Re: Batch Input on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello @Kimaro 

Good question.The Batch Input apps like My Batch Input Sessions (SM35SA) or Batch Input My Logs (SM35PSM) exist mainly for monitoring or reviewing legacy-style input sessions, but you cannot create new Batch Input sessions manually in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In Public Cloud, mass data handling is designed to be done via,

  • Standard Fiori apps for mass changes
  • Import functionality within apps (e.g., Excel upload)
  • SOAP/OData APIs for automated data loads

Batch Input (BDC), as known from On-Premise, is restricted in Public Cloud for system consistency and security reasons, session creation is not exposed to end users.

Refer to the SAP Help documentation below
SAP Help – Data Handling in S/4HANA Public Cloud

SAP Help – Mass Maintenance and Data Import for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
Provides app-based options for mass changes and system-supported imports

Let me know if you need further information

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Express document "Update was canceled" received from author "Jenish Lila
entry.content: So what's your question, how-to add contact person ?

entry.title: Re: Bank Country`s are missing in App F6437 Manage Banks...

entry.title: Re: Configure Governance Rules in SAP Build
entry.content:
Hi @Sidney ,
yes it'll work if you already have Cloud Transport Management setup done for transport, please follow the steps written in this blog, will help to setup governance rules.
Regards,
Diganta

entry.title: Re: Configure Governance Rules in SAP Build
entry.content:
Hi @sshrey14 ,

If you want to do governance rule setup for transport, first you need to setup Cloud Transport Management system for transport the project from one system to other, then you can follow this blog to enable governance rule.

Governance rules are configured at the tenant level. Once the setup is completed for a tenant, it is automatically applicable to all projects within that tenant.
Regards,
Diganta

entry.title: Re: Three new courses for SAP Business One, Web Client are now live on learning.sap.com
entry.content:
Hi Community,

Are those courses replacing the old TB1000, TB1100 and TB1200?

What about those people who were trained in those courses for ##certification? Should they start the new courses from scratch?

entry.title: When running the Simplification Item Check, a consistency check result error appears as shown below.
entry.content:
When running the Simplification Item Check, a consistency check result error appears as shown below. How can we resolve this and proceed? Screenshots are attached.

Check Sub-ID:"DO_SDMI_CHECK->CL_NGC_CORE_SDM_MSEHI_CABN", return code = 12 .
The SDMI (Silent Data Migration) CL_NGC_CORE_SDM_MSEHI_CABN is still initial in client
000. There are 11  relevant entries still to be migrated.
Error: The SDMI CL_NGC_CORE_SDM_MSEHI_CABN is not uptime-capable on the next target
release.
Therefore the SDMI must finish before the upgrade in all clients mentioned above.
The SDMI is registered for S4CORE, current version: 105  , last_uptime_release: 106  .
Target release:108
Explanation "Initial": If the status remains "Initial" over a long time (days) then this
means that there might be a problem with the background job for SDMI execution.
You can check the logs for possible issues (SDM-MON -> Display application log). Normally
the SDMIs should either go to status F (finished) or N (not relevant).
For questions please open a message to the component of the SDMI class CA-CL.
The following select statement can be used to see the relevant data for migration: (check
in correct client)
SELECT * FROM PSDMMSEHICABN WHERE SDM_STATUS < '02'

entry.title: Re: SAP SF Compensation - Best Practice Workflow without...

Best Regards,
Joachim

entry.title: Loading Master and Transactional Data from Datasphere into SAC - Seamless Planing
entry.content:
Hi,

i have created a planning model in SAC, which is also stored and managed in SAP Datasphere.

To load master data and transactional data from Datasphere views into my model, the only method I have found so far is to create OData service connections.

Since I’m working with multiple dimensions, this approach results in a large number of individual connections being visible in SAC, which significantly reduces clarity and usability.

My questions:

  1. Is this currently the only way to integrate master and transactional data from Datasphere views into a SAC planning model that is also stored in Datasphere?

  2. In the context of Seamless Planning, would this OData-based approach be considered appropriate – or are there best practices that suggest otherwise?

Thank you in advance for your support and feedback.

entry.title: Re: Mandatory document attachment tab in Manage Certifica...
entry.content:
Hello @RishikeshMishra 

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, attaching a document in the Manage Certificate Receipts app is optional; there’s no system control to make attachments mandatory before setting the status to "Received."

If your client needs this as a strict requirement, an SAP Influence Request would be needed.

Check below
SAP Help – Quality Certificate Receipt Process

Let me know if you need further assistance

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Overall Performance Rating for Compensation Module - Standard Process
entry.content:
Dear all,

Do the SAP SuccessFactors standard processes specify that the Compensation module must use the Overall Performance Rating that is displayed in the Talent Profile, or does the standard also allow the Compensation module to use an Overall Performance Rating of a different performance form?
So, do the standard processes allow that different Performance Ratings can be used in different modules, or do the different modules have to use the rating, which is displayed in the talent profile?

Many thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: sap public cloud release of2508
entry.content:
Hello @jerrysam 

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, including the latest 2508 release, you can track changes by module like Sourcing & Procurement using:

What's New Viewer – Provides release-specific updates by module, including detailed changes

Roadmap Explorer – Shows upcoming innovations by product and module
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=S4HANA_CLOUD

Test Your Processes App. In your tenant, this helps identify changes based on your active scope items.

You can filter by scope items or specific modules to track only relevant updates for your project.

Let me know if you need help navigating these tools.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: IP-Based Allow-Listing for Applications
entry.content:
For sensitive applications it's often advisable to follow a defense in depth approach. By layering multiple indepenent security controls the risk of a successful attack can be reduced to a minimum.

When the clients that are allowed to connect to a system are well-known and have fixed IP address ranges (such as from a corporate VPN service), applications can first filter for these IP addresses. This is usually done by implementing network ACLs or a firewall that is dedicated to filtering these requests before forwarding them.

In combination with strong authentication like mTLS, this can significantly reduce the risk of a successful attack. You should never rely on IP-based allow-listing alone to protect any resource.

Introducing X-CF-True-Client-IP Header

Up until now this was a difficult task when running as an application on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment as the IP address of the client is not readily available to the app. When a client connects to the platform, it connects to a central load-balancer which handles (among other things) the termination of the TLS connection. When the request reaches the application, the connection is initiated from the load-balancer which uses an internal IP address that cannot be used for such traffic filtering. This gets even more complicated when route-services are in use.

To make building custom filters as easy as possible we have introduced a new request header which will be set in every reqeusts sent to applications: x-cf-true-client-ip. This header contains a single IP address (IPv4 or IPv6) that was used to connect to the platform when the request was initiated.

When the application has a route-service configured, the reported IP address will be the same for the route service and the application, always pointing to the original client which initiated the request.

How to Use X-CF-True-Client-IP

There are two main options to implementing IP-based allow-listing using x-cf-true-client-ip:

  • directly in the main application logic, or
  • in a route service which is then bound to the application route.

In either case you will need to load a pre-defined allow-list, and for every request retrieve the IP from the header and check it against the configured allow-list. To demonstrate the functionality we have implemented a sample route-service with a dummy-app as backend:

Sample Application Using Route-Services

To demonstrate this, we have added two sample applications to our cf-routing-samples. One is the route service itself at ip-allow-listing-route-service/ and the second one is a dummy to act as the protected backend at ip-allow-listing-route-service/ok/.

Deploying the applications can be done via the CF CLI and provided manifest:

# First, clone the repository.
git clone https://github.com/SAP-samples/cf-routing-samples
cd cf-routing-samples/ip-allow-listing-route-service
# Login to CF.
cf login [...]
cf target -o <org> -s <space>
# Push the applications.
cf push --var domain=<domain> --var suffix=<suffix> -f ./manifest.yml

Make sure to replace <domain> with the domain of your environment and provide a unique suffix for <suffix> to prevent collisions on the name of the route. Eventually the CLI should report success and you should see your running applications with 'cf apps'. To ensure our target application works, you can use curl:

curl https://ok-<suffix>.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com

This should print 'ok'. To protect our ok application with the allow-listing route-service we still need to configure them together. The first step is to turn the allow-listing app into a route-service we can bind to. A route service intercepts requests to an app and can decide whether to forward a request to the actual app or not. We use this mechanism to block requests that are originating from a source that is not in the allow-list.

cf create-user-provided-service allow-listing -r https://ip-allow-list-rs-<suffix>.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com

Make sure to provide the same suffix here as you did when pushing the application. Now we can bind the route service to the route of our target application:

cf bind-route-service <domain> --hostname ok-<suffix> allow-listing

To view the finished setup we can use the 'cf routes' command which will output something like this:

space     host                        domain     port   path   protocol   app-protocol   apps                             service instance   options
<space>   ip-allow-list-rs-<suffix>   <domain>                 http       http1          ip-allow-listing-route-service
<space>   ok-<suffix>                 <domain>                 http       http1          ok                               allow-listing

By default, the route-service will allow all traffic from every IPv4 address, so accessing the application should still work:

curl -4 https://ok-<suffix>.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com

If you now delete the single line from the allow-list and push the app again using the same command as before (no need to re-do the service, that persists), you will no longer be able to access it:

error: forbidden: address '192.0.2.5' is not in allow-list

Feel free to take a look at the implementation but please note that this code is only for demonstration purposes and must not be used in production / live systems.

There are some pointers in the code comments on what to do differently, but as always use good judgement and security best practices. The mechanism, by which to provide the specific allow-list to the route service is also left as exercise to the reader.

References

  • SAP-samples/cf-routing-samples
  • Cloud Foundry Route Services

entry.title: Re: newcomer student
entry.content:
Hey Younes,

I’m so glad to hear that you’re interested in starting a local SAP Community group in Casablanca, that’s fantastic news!

To get started, I recommend checking out our Become an Event Organizer page.

It explains what’s involved and how we support you through every step of the way. If you have any questions while going through it, feel free to reach out. I’m happy to help!

Looking forward to seeing the SAP Community grow in Casablanca!

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to delete fs item

entry.title: Re: sap public cloud release of2508
entry.content:
Hello Chuma ,

 

Thankyou for prompt response  , as I struggled to use these things OR links  how can we filter the scopes that are existing as per our activated ones that would be great to know what we use these links as per our business scope

entry.title: Re: SAP Java Buildpack 2: Jakarta-Based, Fresh and New!
entry.content:
Another question:

As part of moving from Docker based to Buildpack based deployment, we are setting extra JAVA options (startup parameters) and security configurations that were set for Docker java applications.

One of the security configurations is networkaddress.cache.ttl.

This was done this way in the Dockerfile of the JDK:

RUN echo "networkaddress.cache.ttl = 30" >> ${JAVA_HOME}/conf/security/java.security

 

What would be the corresponding way to add this security configuration to sap_java_buildpack_jakarta ?

Note: it cannot be set with -D option. This has no effect.

entry.title: Re: Sudden wrong results in app behaviour for previous well working formulas in settings and compone
entry.content:
@timokapanen 

Dear Timo,

I put a timer of 2 seconds on app launch for the screen orientation data variable. it works on SAP Preview and on the app. 

Concerning the sounds written to file (base 64 encoding) written to local folder on app launch, the SAP Preview which a few days ago was not working (not producing sounds) is now producing the sounds without need of adding a delay on app launch but the built app on Test Flight is not producing the sounds for iphone XR, 11 Pro, Ipad M1 and iphone 16 (all models tested).

 It looks like the build encoder is not going through stop points like first going to create all app variables and then loading then only after this first step.  So maybe the app building engine (to get the zip.gpg and then the ipa file) need to be rechecked so that the correct building steps are going one after the other and that for example no uncreated app variable is already trying to be fed a value in the resulting ipa app file. What is strange is that it was working before.

Also it would be weird on programming side to try to guess a delay value so that system is not overwhelmed, it would be a substantial inner fragility of the app building (encoding) system.

Thank you for checking

Best regards,

Chantal

entry.title: Associated GL Company - FI Company
entry.content: When creating consolidation units in Group Reporting, the companies that appear as SAP are the “associated GL companies” and not the FI companies. Could you help me understand why this is happening?

entry.title: Re: Auto printing issue while adding marketting documents
entry.content: Can you explain me how to redirect to virtual printer?.I am probably thinking page setup settings in Crystal report does not affect anything.And how to check for scripts in crystal report.I have check formula fields and nothing related to printing was mentioned

entry.title: Re: Recruiting Groups per legal entity
entry.content:
Hi @alejandroazca

 

Set Permission Rules:
    • Adjust the Permission Rules so that each Recruiting Group has access restrictions based on legal entity identifiers.
    • Configure permission roles in “Admin Center” -> “Manage Permission Roles”, and define the criteria using either a custom field (representing legal entity) or leveraging existing fields like "Department" or "Location".

How can this step be done? In the Role Assignments there is no way to set target criteria = job requisition.legal entity

how can we restrict only by Job Requisition legal entity / BU?

entry.title: Re: Unnecessary objects are visible while applying Leave...
entry.content: Okay, Thank you Arun

entry.title: Re: Error installation ECC6 Ehp7 Windows
entry.content: We are currently experiencing this error as well. How did you resolve it？

entry.title: Re: sap public cloud release of2508
entry.content:
Hello @jerrysam 

Good follow-up, to filter the updates based on your activated scopes, you can use:

As I previously mentioned, the 'Test Your Processes' app in your system displays the scope items you’ve activated and helps track changes or test impacts. In the What’s New Viewer, filter by Scope Items if you know their IDs (e.g., 1XV, 3VX); your project team or CBC setup should provide your scope list.

Unfortunately, there’s no automatic link between What’s New Viewer and your system’s scopes, so manual filtering by known scope items is needed.

If this answer to your question, please accept as the solution, otherwise feel free to contact me anytime

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Introducing Generative AI in SAP SuccessFactors Hands-On Practice Systems
entry.content:
Hello @Markus777 ,

Yes! 🎉 We are currently working on implementing Joule in SuccessFactors practice systems and it will be available on learning.sap.com very soon. 🙂

Regards,

Roxana

entry.title: Re: SAP TechEd 2025: Registration is live – check out our brand new hybrid experience!
entry.content: We are one big happy family @TammyPowlas 😀 - hope to meet you in person some day.

entry.title: Re: This is regarding Poland e-invoicing, Is there any fi...
entry.content:
Note 3279206 - Electronic Document Processing for Poland - KSeF: Implementation Overview basically stipulates that 'You must subscribe to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition service to exchange electronic documents with the tax authority (KSeF). Please get in touch with your SAP representative for further details.'

  • Without GRC, it seems that this would require either custom integration or the use of third-party middleware.
  • What's your version/type of SAP system; S/4HANA, on premise, Cloud, private, public?

entry.title: Re: Enabling automatic document Split for an Outgoing Invoice with 999+ Items while posting an Accou
entry.content: I followed this implementation of METHOD FI_BILL_ISSUE_SPLIT~ACTIVATE_AUTOMATIC_SPLIT and   METHOD FI_BILL_ISSUE_SPLIT~SET_DOCUMENT_TYPE_SUBSEQ but I got this error on my end after I release to Accounting.

entry.title: How to setup Workflow for Enterprise Project
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

How can I setup workflow for Enterprise Project ?

I have setup it already but it doesn't work for Project Control - Enterprise Projects.

Best regards,

Keratiya D.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud: configuration, performance, sizing
entry.content:
SreekanthSurampally

1. Does APRS only configured and used for SAP managed data products? or can this be used in Custom Data products from Z CDS views in S/4 HANA to Datasphere object store?

2. HDLF as the target mentioned in this blog,  is not the one in Datasphere HDLF right?

3. In this SAP managed data products integration using APRS, Datasphere replication flows are not used right?

4. When Intelligent/Insight app is activated, the APRS initial and delta process is initiated from S/4 and load the data into HDLF of BDC Foundation service, then why an integration Datasphere space is created with replication flows to load the data for these data products?

1. In this blog APRS described  as part of BDC product. So, as I know currently BDC could replicated only SAP provided data products. Better refer to BDC documentation.

2. BUT, APRS also used in Datasphere when source is S/4 HANA system (target BDC-specific part in such case is not relevant). So, from this point of view, configuration and optimization of source system is the same.

3. As I mentioned in (2) Datasphere also used APRS when source is S/4 HANA system

4. Datasphere and BDC is 2 different SAP products. In both cases APRS used on source side. The subscription process is different and follow up data processing in both cases also difference.

entry.title: The results of the data action "XXXXXXX" could not be published on version "yyyy".
entry.content:
Hello, 

One user getting below error message when publish the data action from story. The data action is having 3 steps which copy data from different model.

All steps are running fine, but when publish step comes then it shows below error

The results of the data action "XXXXXXX" could not be published on version "yyyy". You don't have the resources available to publish this version. Please try again later." 

The strange part is that, when I run the data action I am able to do it without any issue. I am the owner of that model.

There is a KBA from SAP, but I am not getting as what to change here because I am able to run the data action without any issue.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003618840

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud: configuration, performance, sizing
entry.content:
albertosimeoni
can APRS be used to replicate data outside SAP / HDLF ? 

APRS is not separate standalone product. It's used as part of numbers other products: SLT, DataIntelligence, DataSphere, BDC. In all cases where product replicate data from CDS View or SAP Table from S/4 HANA system.

entry.title: Re: Rise With SAP - The FUE Game
entry.content: None of these appear to relate to MIGO (not directly at least)? Which auth object is the issue?

entry.title: Re: Wrong behaviour of cl_gui_alv_grid or wrong usage?
entry.content:
I do not agree. ALV is the UI layer. If you offer values in an F4 help and then trick the user with an error message, that's a wrong UI design. And that's implemented.
Now the special cases: 
If a possible value selected by the user does not fit in all combinations to the complete data set values, then of course an error message is required. But it is triggered by business logic. The generic UI logic cannot decide. 
If a possible value selected by the user never fits to the complete data set values, then the Business logic must pimp the value set offered by the value help.

entry.title: Clarification on TDS Threshold Configuration in SAP S/4HANA Public
entry.content:
We would like to confirm whether TDS threshold-based deduction (as per Indian statutory requirements) can be configured and automated in our SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment.

Specifically, we are looking to configure:

  • Minimum invoice-level threshold (e.g., ₹30,000)

  • Cumulative yearly threshold (e.g., ₹1,00,000 per vendor per financial year)

  • Automatic TDS deduction once thresholds are breached

  • TDS applicability based on section-wise rates (e.g., 194C, 194J, etc.)

Kindly confirm the below:

  1. Can these thresholds be maintained in the WHT Type/Code configuration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

  2. Is cumulative threshold tracking supported for each vendor annually?

  3. Do we need to activate any additional scope items or business configurations?

  4. Could you share the list of relevant SSCUIs or apps for maintaining this setup?

Appreciate your support on this. Please let us know if any additional steps or approvals are required from our side.

entry.title: IEQUIPMENTATTR cds view does not load delta properly
entry.content:
Hello Guys,

I've a problem to make delta extraction on the CDS view IEQUIPMENTATTR on my datasource 0I_EQUIPMENTDATA.

 

 

 

 

 

 

When I load full load everything seems to work fine but when I run it in delta mode it runs on error.

It says that no more data available to load but since it is an init it should load more rows definitely.

It seems to me that it runs on error on the source side but can’t seems to find any issue.
Could you please propose some solution/checks that would solve the issue ?

Thanks for all the hints help

entry.title: Re: User SAPSR3DB does not exist during an SAP Netweaver...
entry.content:
SWPM can do the ORACLE installation by itself on an automated way. 

But, if ORACLE DB is installed manually before the SWPM, then you need to perform a extended list of customizations as recommended by SAP.

Please look into the SAP official documentation: 

  • 2799900 - Central Technical Note for Oracle Database 19c - SAP for Me
  • 2660018 - Oracle Database Software Installation on Microsoft Windows - SAP for Me
  • Installation of SAP Systems Based on the Application Server ABAP of SAP NetWeaver 7.3 EHP1 to 7.52 on Windows: Oracle

entry.title: SAP BTP Web IDEの画面に表示されているJava Build Pack1廃止の警告の件について
entry.content:
各位
お疲れ様です。
ご存じの方がいらっしゃればなにとぞご教授ください。

SAP BTP Web IDEのJava Build Pack1が廃止されるという話が以下のブログに記載されていました。
Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE 

今日が廃止対象の6月末ではあるのですが、こちらは以下の認識で合っておりますでしょうか？
・Cloud Foundryへのビルドができない
・現在すでにCloud Foundryへビルドされているものが動作しなくなるわけではない

また、Web IDE上の警告で以下の警告になっておりました。
ブログ記載の通り6月末が対象かと思っていたのですが、期間が変更になったということなのでしょうか？

原文：
Please note that on September 2025, java tools in sap web ide will be removed due to discontinuation of the java buildpack1 from the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment. For more information, see the Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE blog post.

翻訳：
2025年9月、SAP BTP Cloud Foundry環境におけるJavaビルドパック1のサポート終了に伴い、SAP Web IDEのJavaツールも削除されますのでご注意ください。詳しくは、ブログ記事「SAP Web IDEにおけるJavaツールの廃止」をご覧ください。

ご協力よろしくお願いいたします。
#SAPBTP
#BuildPack1
#SAPWebIDE

entry.title: Re: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud: configuration, performance, sizing
entry.content:
Sorry but this do not answer my question:

ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud introduces a new technology called ABAP Push Replication Service (APRS). APRS pushes the data of CDS views, which are part of Data Products, into HANA Data Lake Files (HDLFS).

From What I understand replication flows (used in normal spaces of Datasphere) do use APE (Abap Pipeline engine).
in that case is the replication flow that read from S/4 source system, it reads the RDB tables (PULL).

Due to limitations of RFC/ODP/ reserved API for SAP, external tools can not extract CDS views in init+delta mode (we know OData exists but it is slow and with some bugs, for example datetime columns not supported / crash errors).

In APRS: is the source applicaton that do a PUSH of data to HDLF? (no SAP private API call from external tool involved?)

Can APRS be used to push data to another object store ? (non SAP managed object Store?).

Thanks,
Alberto

entry.title: SAP public cloud edition. CO assigment is not available for mass update in posting area 2610
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I'm trying to add the COKEY CO Acc assignment field in the configuration but this field is not available for a mass update in posting area 2610 in SAP public cloud edition...

Any suggestion to update the posting area 2610 in a mass way?

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Problem activating Support user via SLD or RSP
entry.content:
We encounter the same problem. After activating the support user in SLD we cannot login with the support user in SLD.

The listed SAP Notes are not accessible yet. Do you have any updates on this matter?

entry.title: Re: Integrate Commerce cloud to multiple accounts of Emarsys
entry.content: Unfortunately it is not possible as Emarsys integration plugin checks whether there is already existing integration with target Commerce Cloud environment and do not allow to make another integration.

entry.title: How to load Data From ECC to S4 Hana Finance for Group Reporting
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are implementing GR for Priviate Edition. GR system is not yet ready, how ever we need to show one live cycle to customer. our source system is ECC and NoN integrated sytems(units). 

My questin is: How can I get the data from ECC to GR system.? is ther any program or methodology / procesdure to place the data into Group reporting system. 

Thanks,
MS Rao.

entry.title: Field ID is empty in Tax Category of TaxSubtotal
entry.content:
Hi,

Kindly how could I maintain Field ID in  Tax Category of TaxSubtotal? Is this in /n/AIF/VMAP -> Define Value Mapping? If yes, what could I maintain?

BR

entry.title: Re: Output tables in Kmeans
entry.content: @ChristophMorgen could you please provide me with more information on this? How can I delete temporary tables if they are not saved?

entry.title: Re: CDS : How to define Hierarchy View
entry.content: Yes we can use it on Prem also. S/4 HANA AP 2023.

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud release June 2025 Round-up
entry.content:
Dear SAP HANA Cloud Enthusiasts,

We are thrilled to share a comprehensive collection of the latest videos, blogposts, and resources from the SAP HANA Cloud Q2 2025 release.

 

What’s New Teaser

Explore the latest innovations in SAP HANA Cloud with Lead Product Manager Thomas Hammer! In this exciting teaser, he shares his favorite features from the newest release.

Watch it now on YouTube.

What’s New blogpost

Intrigued by the teaser? Explore our "What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud in June 2025" blog post for an in-depth look and find valuable links for further insights.

Read it here!

What's New Webinar

Prefer watching over reading? Our 'What’s New' webinar is just for you! Join our Product experts for an in-depth view of the latest features. Watch anytime here!

 

Additional information


In addition to our regular release updates, make sure to check the following valuable content provided by our team:

  • New Machine Learning and AI features in SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q2 by @ChristophMorgen 
  • SAP HANA Cloud Availability in SAP Sovereign Cloud and SAP NS2 by @isomesan
  • Explore more on Calculation View features in this blogpost by @jan_zwickel 
Don’t miss out on all the content and remember to follow us in the SAP HANA Community.

Remember to check our content following the # whatsnewinsaphanacloud tag: here

Don’t forget to subscribe and follow SAP HANA Cloud on YouTube to always stay up-to-date regarding the most recent innovations in SAP HANA Cloud.
 
All the best,

Andrea on behalf of the SAP HANA Cloud team

#SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database   #SAP HANA Cloud  

#whatsnewinsaphanacloud

entry.title: Re: How to activate Transaction Builder in SAP MDK ( not...
entry.content:
Hello

You can simply set TransactionID in the RequestOptions of CreateEntity/UpdateEntity/DeleteEntity to group multiple independent operations into a Change Set without using the ChangeSet action. There should be no need to enable Transaction Builder in this case.

A change set will always be sent as a batch, but because your operations will be grouped in a change set, technically, if one of the operation failed, your backend will (or should) rollback all prior successful operations that’s in the same change set request that’s being executed.

Regards

Ming

entry.title: Re: Custom tokens in Onboarding
entry.content: Hi Karthick,
